name: BTC Inventory Reset Scaleout v1
thesis: BTC 4h is the best-fit similar-asset lane for a flush-and-reclaim inventory reset concept with staged exits. The edge should come from overextended inventory normalizing back through value and continuing enough to monetize multiple exit slices.
invalidation_condition: Abort if the reclaim setup remains rare, if direction confirmation conflicts with the reset logic, or if the later scale-out legs fail to overcome cost drag.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A meaningful flush resets short-term inventory, price reclaims value, and continuation follows for several bars before fading.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: BTC often expresses reclaim-after-flush behavior more cleanly than ETH while staying close enough in structure to validate the concept on the same 4h canvas.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is the right similar-asset lane because the reset needs enough amplitude to matter, but still benefits from staged scale-out rather than a single fixed take-profit.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the reclaim only generates a shallow bounce and not continuation, later exit slices will never pay for the idea.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries populate but the runner underperforms, reduce runner ambition before changing the reclaim trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reclaim and DI confirmation align after the reset
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reset thresholds rather than rare extreme events
